original: Gothic War (535-552): The Ostrogoths, under King Totila, conquer Rome after a long siege, by bribing the Isaurian garrison
Caliphate of Cordoba is established by Emir Abd-al-Rahman III
The Council of Nablus is held, establishing the earliest surviving written laws of the Crusader Kingdom of Jerusalem
Floods in Northern Netherlands after storm, thousands killed
A great storm tide in the North Sea destroys the German island of Strand and the city of Rungholt
The Medici family is appointed official banker of the Papacy
The first grammar of a modern language, Spanish, is presented to Queen Isabella.
English Reformation parliament's 2nd sitting
Thomas Howard, 4th Duke of Norfolk is tried and convicted of high treason for his part in the Ridolfi plot to restore Catholicism in England
English parliament passes laws against Catholicism
Infamous sword duel between George Villiers, Duke of Buckingham and Francis Talbot, Earl of Shrewsbury over Anna, Duchess of Shrewsbury and mistress of Buckingham. The Earl is mortally wounded.
The Act of Union is ratified by the Scottish Parliament

Hoax article advertising fictitious theatrical performer "The Bottle Conjuror" drew huge crowds to the Haymarket Theatre, London, whose inevitable non-appearance caused a riot. It's alleged the Duke of Montagu perpetrated the fiasco to win a bet.
Britain & Prussia sign Treaty of Westminster to respect each other's European territories
The British capture Pondicherry, India, from the French
Continental Congress approves enlistment of free blacks
Vermont declares independence from New York
Battle of Cape St Vincent: British fleet under Admiral Sir George Rodney defeats Spanish squadron under Don Juan de Lángara
French army under Pichegru occupies Utrecht, Netherlands
Peninsular War: The British defeat the French at the Battle of Corunna
Godert van der Capellen, becomes Governor-General of Dutch East Indies
John C. Frémont is appointed Governor of the new California Territory
Hartley Colliery disaster in Northumberland, England, results in 204 deaths
-Aug 23rd) Cruise of CSS Florida
Heavy fighting takes place near Dandridge, Tennessee
Munitions magazine explodes at Fort Fisher, North Carolina, killing or injuring 200 soldiers, carelessness of drunken soldiers blamed.
San Francisco Dramatic Chronicle starts
Refrigerator car patented by William Davis, a fish dealer in Detroit
Virginia becomes 8th state readmitted to US after Civil War
Jefferson Long of Georgia sworn in as 2nd black congressman
Color organ (for light shows) patented, by Bainbridge Bishop
Captain Burago with a squadron of Russian Imperial army dragoons liberates Plovdiv from Ottoman rule
January record 13" of snow falls in NYC (broken Jan 7, 1996)

The Windom Resolution by Minnesota Senator William Windom encourages black migration out of the South, helping trigger the "Exodus of 1879". 6,000 southern blacks flee Louisiana, Mississippi and Texas for Kansas.
Pendleton Act creates basis of US Civil Service system
Quebec Rugby Football Union forms
Cliff House damaged when powder cargo of schooner "Parallel" explodes
128°F (53°C), Cloncurry, Queensland (Australian record)
Defeat of Cymru Fydd at South Wales Liberal Federation AGM, Newport, Monmouthshire.
The United States Senate accepts the Anglo-German treaty of 1899 in which the United Kingdom renounces its claims to the Samoan islands
The US gains control over Tutuila in Samoa and several smaller Pacific islands
Baseball outfielder Frank Huelsman completes eighth transaction in 8 months
Stanley Cup, Dey's Arena, Ottawa, Ontario: Ottawa Senators rout Dawson City (Yukon), 23-2 for 2-0 series sweep; most lopsided challenge playoff game in history; Frank McGee 14 goals
-Apr 13] Conference of Algeciras (about Morocco)
Pinnacles National Monument, California established
Antarctic explorers Douglas Mawson, Edgeworth David and Alistair Mackay reach south magnetic pole as part of the Nimrod Expedition
Pandora becomes 1st 2-man sailboat to round Cape Horn west to east
British House of Commons accepts Home Rule for Ireland (but the Great War gets in the way of it happening)
Congress authorizes $1 & $50 Panama-Pacific Intl Expo gold coin

"Zimmermann Telegram" is sent from Germany to Mexico, offering promise of Texas, Arizona, and New Mexico being given to Mexico in return for joining World War I if the US enters on the Allied side; British intelligence intercepts the communication and partially deciphers it by the next day. It's release in March shifts US public opinion in favor of war against Germany.
The Greek Government accepts reparations for Allied losses sustained in recent actions in Greece
Austria and Germany are disrupted by strikes as people express impatience with leaders continuing the war
First assembly of the League of Nations is held in Paris
Georgia declares independence
Zeta Phi Beta Sorority Incorporated is founded at Howard University
Ralph Vaughan Williams' "Pastoral Symphony" premieres in London, with Adrian Boult conducting
Cricket's Bodyline Tour: Australian batsman Bert Oldfield's skull fractured by delivery bowled by Englishman Harold Larwood during the third test in Adelaide
1st photo finish camera installed at Hialeah Race track in Hialeah, Florida
Spanish socialists, communists and anarchists form Unidad Popular
Daily newspaper comic strip "Superman" debuts
The Irish Republican Army (IRA) begins a 14 month long bombing campaign in England
War Department forms first Army Air Corps squadron for black cadets
William Knudsen becomes 1st civilian appointed a general in US army
-60°F (-51°C), Island Park Dam, Idaho (state record)
1st US air raid on Ambon
German 2nd SS-Panzer division evacuates Charkow
Red Army recaptures Pitomnik airport at Stalingrad

US Coast Guard pilot Stewart Graham first to fly a helicopter from a ship, taking off for a surveillance mission from British freighter SS Daghestan, part of a convoy sailing from New York to Liverpool, England
Scottish 52nd land division and 1st Commando brigade-assault at Heinsberg
US 1st & 3rd army meet at Houffalise
Vincent Aurial elected President of France
35 Haganah members are ambushed & killed in Gush Etzyon
KNBH (now KNBC) TV channel 4 in Los Angeles, CA (NBC) 1st broadcast
WTOP (now WUSA) TV channel 9 in Washington, D.C. (CBS) 1st broadcast
Belgium, Luxembourg & Netherlands recognize Israel
Viet Minh offensive against Hanoi
World's largest gas pipeline opens (Brownsville Texas, to 134th St, New York City)
New Dutch bible translation finished
Egyptian Premier General Naguib disbands all political parties
KXLY TV channel 4 in Spokane, Washington (ABC/CBS) begins broadcasting
3 US B-52 bombers leave California for 1st non-stop around the world flights
Cavern Club opens on Matthews Street in Liverpool, England, home of many performances by The Beatles
William Gibson's "Two for the Seesaw" premieres in NYC
Russian espionage ring detected in Great Britain
Shooting begins on "Dr No" the first James Bond film
Suit accuses NYC Board of Education uses "racial quotas"
Los Angeles night club and music venue The Whiskey A-Go-Go opens (inducted into Rock and Roll Hall of Fame 2006)
AL owners vote 9-1 against Charlie Finley moving KC A's to Louisville
"Outer Limits" last airs on ABC-TV
The Searchers' "Love Potion Number 9" peaks at #3
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Chicago is granted an NBA franchise to be called the Bulls; become the 3rd NBA franchise in the city, after the Chicago Stags (1946–50) and Chicago Packers/Zephyrs (now Washington Wizards)
Harold R Perry becomes 2nd black Roman Catholic bishop in US
Johnson Aguiyi-Ironsi seizes power in the chaos caused by a military coup and becomes the Nigerian Head of State
1st black government installed in Bahamas led by Lynden Pindling as leader of the Progressive Liberal Party
Lucius Amerson, becomes 1st southern (Alabama) African American sheriff in 20th century
Jay Allen's "Prime of Miss Jean Brodie" premieres in NYC
The Youth International Party is founded.
Jan Palach, a Czech student, self-immolates in protest after the Soviet invasion of Czechoslovakia
Soviet Soyuz 4 & Soyuz 5 perform 1st transfer of crew in space
AAU player Steve Myers makes a basketball field goal of 92'3½" from out of bounds, Tacoma-it shouldn't have counted, but was allowed
Buckminster Fuller receives the Gold Medal award from the American Institute of Architects
Inaugural Buffalo Sabres owners Seymour H. Knox III and Northrup Knox hire former Toronto Maple Leafs general manager and head coach Punch Imlach in the same capacities for their new NHL franchise
NFL realigns into 3 divisions (down from 4)
Dutch speed skater Atje Keulen-Deelstra becomes European Allround ladies champion, for the first of three consecutive years
Super Bowl VI, Tulane Stadium, New Orleans, LA: Dallas Cowboys beat Miami Dolphins, 24-3; MVP: Roger Staubach, Dallas, QB
NBC presents 440th and final showing of western TV series "Bonanza"
Scientists at Corning Glass granted U.S. patent #3,711,262 for optical fiber (invented August 1970) - beginning a telecommunications revolution
USSR's Lunakhod 2 begins radio-controlled exploration of Moon
Landslide kills 9 in Canyonville, Oregon
"Donny & Marie" [Osmond] musical variety show premieres on ABC TV
Peter Frampton released platinum live album "Frampton Comes Alive"
Cap's H Monahan scored on 2nd penalty shot against Islanders
Soyuz 27 returns to Earth
Protestant gunmen shoot & wound Bernadette Devlin McAliskey & husband
"Playboy" announces end of stapling centerfolds
First meeting of the Internet Engineering Task Force.
Police arrested 3 IRA-terrorists in Amsterdam
Jimmy "the Greek" Snyder fired from CBS for racial remarks
NFL St Louis Cardinals announce move to Phoenix
USSR announces plan for 2-yr manned mission to Mars
2 Bank of Credit & Commerce members plea guilty to money laundering
Chapultepec Peace Accords are signed in Mexico City which ends the 12-year Salvadoran Civil War
Scott skates world record 1000m (1:12.54)
UPN (Universal-Parmount Network) begins telecasting (WWOR in NYC)
Anthony Stuart takes ODI hat-trick, Aus v Pakistan, MCG
A fuel supply tanker runs aground off the island of San Cristobal, causing an 'ecological disaster'
US President Bill Clinton awards former President Theodore Roosevelt a posthumous Medal of Honor for his service in the Spanish–American War
The Space Shuttle Columbia takes off for mission STS-107 which would be its final one. Columbia disintegrates 16 days later on re-entry.
At 66 years old, Romanian university lecturer Adriana Illiescu becomes the oldest birth mother in the world
24 people die after three car bombs exploded in Idlib Governorate, Syria
25 people are killed after an apartment block collapses in Alexandria, Egypt
A four day occupation of an Algerian BP facility by Amenas militants begins, killing 48 hostages
First ever flower grown in space - a zinnia aboard the International Space Station using NASA Veggie system
Australian Bureau of Meteorology tweets last four days were the country's hottest on record with Tarcoola, South Australia, reaching 49 C (120 F), Port Augusta 48.5 C (119 F)
Egyptian city Cairo is engulfed by a sandstorm turning it orange
Explosion by a suicide bomber aimed at US-led coalition forces at a market in Manjib, Syria, kills about 18 including four Americans
Golden State Warriors break 2 franchise records in 142-111 win over Denver Nuggets; in 1st quarter, record 10 three-point field goals on way to franchise best 51 points in the period

Houston guard James Harden becomes first player to score 55+ points in back-to-back NBA games since Wilt Chamberlain (1962); scores 58 in 145-142 OT loss to Brooklyn; 18 straight games with 30+ points
UN says at least 890 killed in ethnic violence in the western Democratic Republic of Congo in December, with 465 building destroyed, delaying the presidential election
Former Sacramento State softball player Alyssa Nakken becomes first woman to hold a coaching position on a MLB staff when named an assistant by the San Francisco Giants

The MacLean brothers, Ewan (27), Jamie (26), and Lachlan (21)  of Scotland, arrive in Antigua after rowing a boat across the Atlantic Ocean in record 35 days, 9 hours, and 9 minutes; in addition to making the fastest three-man crossing, they are also the youngest, and first sibling trio
10 Nepali climbers become the first to reach the summit of K2 in winter on the China-Pakistan border
India begin the vaccination of its 1.3 billion people for COVID-19
Italy's most wanted mobster Matteo Messina Denaro (61) arrested at a private cancer clinic in Palermo, Sicily after being on the run for 30 years
Iran carries out a missile attack on militant group Jaish al-Adl in Balochistan, Pakistan, killing two children and angering Pakistan

Actor and director Justin Baldoni countersues actressBlake Livelyfor $400 million for defamation and extortion, over their film "It Ends With Us", after Lively earlier sued for him for harassment and a smear campaign
The titleAugustusis bestowed upon Gaius Julius CaesarOctavianby the Roman Senate
Christopher Columbusleaves the New World and sets sail for Spain
Portuguese navigatorPedro Álvares Cabraland 6 ships set off from Kannur, India, on their return voyage to Lisbon
Ivan the Terrible, aged 17, crowns himself the 1st tsar of Moscow
Holy Roman EmperorCharles Vabdicates his role as King of Spain and appoints his sonPhilip II of Spain
The first edition of "El ingenioso hidalgo Don Quijote de la Mancha" (Book One of Don Quixote) byMiguel de Cervantesis published in Madrid
French astronomerCharles Messiercatalogs M41 (galactic cluster in Canis Major)
French KingLouis XVIsentenced to death by the National Convention during theFrench Revolution
Charles Darwinlands at Porto Prayo in the Cape Verde islands, the first landing of his HMS Beagle voyage
GeneralWilliam T. Shermanissues Field Order #15, redistributing confiscated Southern land to newly freed Black families in forty-acre plots
American scholarJohn Dewey's essay "My Pedagogic Creed" appears in School Journal
WriterMaxim Gorkyreturns to Russia from Capri after being granted an amnesty
Eleftherios Venizelosbecomes Prime Minister of Greece (4th time)
British Government ofStanley Baldwinresigns
Hans Lutherbecomes Chancellor of Germany.
Australian cricket batting prodigyDon Bradmanscores 223 v West Indies in 3rd Test in Brisbane; most runs scored by a player in a Test match in Australia in one day
First jazz concert held at Carnegie Hall, performed byBenny Goodmanand his band
GeneralEisenhowertakes command of Allied Invasion Force in London
Adolf Hitlermoves into the Fuhrerbunker, his underground bunker in Berlin, where he lives until his suicide April 30
US Standard Board clearsStan Musialto get an $85,000 salary
EgyptianPresident Nasserpledges to reconquer Palestine

Detroit right wingGordie Howescores a goal and assist in his 888th career game, a 3-1 win at home over Chicago; becomes NHL's all-time leading scorer, with 947 points passing Montreal's Maurice Richard
Khrushchevclaims to have a 100-megaton nuclear bomb
Tennessee Williams' play "Milk Train Doesn't Stop Here Anymore" premieres in America on Broadway, NY
Chicago Black Hawks' right wingBobby Hullscores 4 goals (his 17th career hat trick) in a 6-5 loss to NY Rangers; becomes the first player in team history to score 300 goals
4 months after leading a coup against monarchy,Muammar Gaddafitakes over rule of Libya
Curt Floodfiles a civil lawsuit challenging baseball's reserve clause
Dutch speed skaterArd Schenkskates world record 1500m (1:58.7)
Novel "Jaws" byPeter Benchleyis published by Doubleday
BBC landmark nature series "Life on Earth" presented byDavid Attenboroughfirst shown on BBC One
ShahMohammad Reza Pahlaviflees Iran for Egypt during the Iranian Revolution
Paul McCartneyis arrested at Tokyo International Airport for possession of marijuana; he is sent to jail for nine days before being deported
BoxerLeon Spinksis mugged, his assailants even take his gold teeth
John Lennon's single "Woman" is posthumously released in the UK
Edmonton's rookie goaltenderGrant Fuhrhas his 23-game unbeaten streak end with a 7-1 Oilers' defeat in Toronto
Paul &Linda McCartneyarrested in Barbados for possession of cannabis
Czech dissidentVáclav Havelis arrested in Prague for taking part in demonstrations against the communist government
George Harrison's single "Got My Mind Set On You" tops the Billboard charts; the cover song was written by Rudy Clark and originally recorded by James Ray in 1962
6th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees: LaVern Baker; The Byrds; John Lee Hooker; The Impressions;Wilson Pickett; Jimmy Reed; Ike andTina Turner; Howlin' Wolf; Dave Bartholomew; and Ralph Bass

Eric Claptonperforms for an episode of MTV "Unplugged" at Bray Film Studios in Windsor, England. Recording won six Grammy Awards, and became Clapton's bestselling album, and the bestselling live album of all time
Congolese PresidentLaurent-Désiré Kabilais assassinated in his office by one of his own bodyguards
UN Security Council unanimously establishes an arms embargo and freezes assets ofOsama bin Laden, Al-Qaida, and the members of the Taliban
EllenJohnson-Sirleafis sworn in as Liberia's new president. She becomes Africa's first female elected head of state.
French politicianMarine Le Penbecomes the leader of the National Front Party

Golden State guardStephen Curryfirst player in NBA history to hit 8+ three-point FGs in 3 consecutive games; goes 9-of- 17 three's in Warriors' 147-140 win over New Orleans; teams combine for NBA record 43 3-pointers
UK Prime MinisterTheresa Maywins vote of no confidence in her government 325 to 306
Armin Laschet is elected leader of Germany's Christian Democrats (CDU), to replace long-serving leaderAngela Merkel
The 18th Amendment to the US Constitution, authorizing the prohibition of alcohol, is ratified by a majority of US states
US vice admiral Bellinger warns of an assault on Pearl Harbor
Edmund Crouchback, English prince and son of Henry III of England, born in London (d. 1296)
René of Anjou, King of Naples (1435-42) and last heir of the House of Anjou, born in Château d'Angers, Angers, Anjou, France (d. 1480)
Johannes Schöner, German polymath, astronomer and cartographer (Schöner globes), born in Karlstadt am Main, Lower Franconia, Bavaria (d. 1547)
Anthony Denny, English knight of the shire and confidant of King Henry VIII of England, born in England (d. 1559)
Alexander Adriaenssen, Flemish painter known for his still lifes of fish and game pieces, born in Antwerp (d. 1661)
